claim: The root `CLAUDE.md` shall contain a DOMAIN-MAP block listing every `domains/<name>/` with id, description, goals, director, path, atoms-count.
why: The framework operator must be able to discover all domains without reading each domain's graph. The DOMAIN-MAP block is generated by RenderDomainMapBlock (internal/generator/claudemd.go) wrapped in the DOMAIN-MAP sentinel block by the root CLAUDE.md renderer, so it cannot drift from the actual directory layout (R-deterministic-generation): the renderer walks the domains/ directory on disk, loads each domain's graph.json for the SETTLED atom count, and looks up presentation fields (id, description, goals, director) from the code-level knownDomainManifests map. atoms-count gives the operator a quick load-budget estimate (R-context-budget-rule). The on-disk manifest.json carries only self_hosting, so the description/goals/director fields are sourced from knownDomainManifests (with em-dash fallback for unknown domains) rather than the manifest file; a future create_domain tool would persist them on disk. ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestRenderDomainMapBlock_KnownDomainCarriesAllSixFields in internal/generator/claudemd_coverage_test.go mechanically verifies this claim against the generated crystal on the fixture graph. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.
